title: podman logs -f continues indefinitely on a stopped container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**
/kind bug

**Description**
Running `podman logs -f` on a stopped container run indefinitely and does not exit
This is a regression of Issue https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/435 and PR https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/437
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run a container which exits immediately
```
podman run -d --name hello-world hello-world
```

2. Follow logs of stopped container
```
podman logs -f hello-world
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Command does not exit; hangs indefinitely

**Describe the results you expected:**
Command should exit.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
The expected behavior of Docker is to exit when following a log of a stopped container

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.0-dev
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.3
Git Commit:   d857275901e8c1ea7515360631e5894018e17f30
Built:        Fri Jun  5 17:00:00 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


comments.body:
Did some extended testing and confirm the issue exists in older versions of podman in addition to the one reported:
* podman 1.6.4 - Hangs
* podman 1.9.3 - Hangs
Just for grins, what does Docker do?

Docker exits properly. Podman used to have this same functionality and was
fixed in a bug 2 years ago, but the log.go code was completely rewritten so
it is a regression.

On Tue, Jun 9, 2020, 07:38 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Just for grins, what does Docker do?
>
> —

> .
>
@jgallucci32 Interested on working on a fix and opening a PR?

@rhatdan I'm not too familiar with the code base and only have a test environment created on Fedora Rawhide. Not sure where in the code I would begin to look for this functionality but I would need a way to build/test before I submitted any PR for this. Any suggesitons?

/reopen

This issue was originally fixed in #6591 but has been reverted in #6644 to address problems with tests failing.

@jgallucci32: Reopened this issue.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/6531#issuecomment-645608677):

>

If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
